column1: # cvvdp Mode B P2.1b + P2.1c — body+halo CSF + strip-major-outer (2026-05-27)
# Source code state: HEAD after P2.1b (perf(cvvdp-gpu): P2.1b — extend
# per-strip CSF helper to body+halo dispatch) + P2.1c (perf(cvvdp-gpu):
# P2.1c — invert outer loop to strip-major for k < k_split). Hardware:
# water-cooled 7950X + NVIDIA RTX 5070 (CUDA driver 596.21
# Measurement: examples/mem_mode_b_vs_full.rs (subprocess-per-cell;
# child process holds GPU buffers for CHILD_HOLD_MS=400 while parent
# samples nvidia-smi memory.used delta).
# columns: size_label
# JOD parity vs Full mode at both sizes: |diff| = 0.000000 (bit-identical).
# Key result: at 4096² Mode B (h_body=256) now sits at -18.2% vs Full
# at the nvsmi-driver level (3457 MiB vs 4225 MiB). The estimator
# predicts -82.2% (565 MiB vs 3178 MiB master-baseline) — the gap is
# the dominant cubecl pool over-allocation + full-image t_p_*/m_*
# transients which are NOT YET shrunk to per-strip in P2.1b/c. Those
# shrinks land in P2.4-P2.8.
# Wall-time: Mode B is FASTER than Full at 4096² (4.67s vs 5.61s)
# because P2.1c's strip-major-outer with upfront transient
# allocation avoids the cubecl pool churn of today's lazy-per-level
# allocation under level-major-outer. Well within MODE_SELECTION.md's
# ≤3× strip vs full budget; in fact Mode B is now faster.
